title: Contribution Claim

statuteRef.label: Special time limit for claiming contribution

questions.label: Date of judgment or settlement
questions.helpText: The date on which judgment was given against the contributor, or the date on which the amount of the contributor's liability was agreed (i.e. the date of settlement). Under s.10 Limitation Act 1980, the 2-year period runs from this date.

questions.label: Was the claimant under a disability at the date of accrual?
questions.helpText: Under the Limitation Act 1980, a person is under a disability if they are a minor (under 18) or lack capacity within the meaning of the Mental Capacity Act 2005.

questions.label: Date disability ceased
questions.helpText: The date the claimant turned 18, or the date capacity was regained.

questions.label: Was there fraud, deliberate concealment, or mistake?
questions.helpText: Under s.32 Limitation Act 1980, if the defendant deliberately concealed relevant facts, the limitation period may be postponed until the claimant discovered or could with reasonable diligence have discovered the concealment.

questions.label: Date of discovery
questions.helpText: The date the claimant discovered, or could with reasonable diligence have discovered, the fraud, concealment, or mistake.

notes: Contribution claims are governed by the Civil Liability (Contribution) Act 1978. The right to claim contribution arises when two or more persons are liable for the same damage.
The 2-year period is notably short. The date of accrual is the date of judgment or the date the contributor's liability is quantified by agreement.
Where contribution is sought by way of additional claim in existing proceedings, the rules of court may impose additional procedural time limits.
